cases.title: Rank real gaps when coverage and weak tests are both visible
cases.user_request: Find the highest-value test gaps in this workspace, fix the best small batch, and strengthen any obviously weak tests you touch.

notes: Coverage artifacts and runnable tests exist.
Some code paths are technically covered by weak tests with shallow assertions.

static_checks: Treats weak tests as real gaps instead of only listing uncovered files.
review_questions: Does the response distinguish missing coverage from weak existing tests?
Are the selected fixes or proposed changes small enough for an incremental pass?
Does the validation plan stay narrow and realistic for the touched surfaces?

cases.title: Stay honest when coverage reports are unavailable
cases.user_request: Improve test coverage in this repo, but use what's already here and don't invent a new framework.

notes: No trusted coverage artifact is available.
The repository has established test naming, helpers, and package scripts.

artifact_requirements: evidence-backed prioritization despite missing coverage report
static_checks: Does not pretend a coverage percentage exists when the artifact is missing.
review_questions: Does the response reuse local test conventions instead of suggesting a new framework?
Does it stay honest about the missing coverage signal while still ranking plausible opportunities?
Are the proposed improvements grounded in the inspected repo surface rather than generic test advice?

cases.title: Model the workflow as a recurring incremental pass
cases.user_request: Set up the workflow I could run weekly: find the top test gaps, use subagents where safe, and leave a ranked backlog for the next pass.

notes: The workspace is large enough that only a small batch should be touched in one run.
Some packages are cleanly separable, but others share helpers and fixtures.

static_checks: Leaves a ranked backlog instead of pretending the whole workspace should be fixed in one pass.
review_questions: Does the response keep the run bounded enough for a recurring schedule?
Does it use parallel or subagent work only where write boundaries are clearly separated?
Would the resulting workflow improve confidence over time instead of just inflating coverage metrics?
